name: Joseph Hoang
title: Bestie package arrived
date: Reviewed in the United States on July 11, 2026
body: QD sent me this book personally since he's my bestie. I haven't read it yet.

name: YoCherng Lyang
title: Too high level for me
date: Reviewed in the United States on July 8, 2026
body: Bro, this book is too high level for me. I'm dead

name: Tan Mai
title: Already 7.0
date: Reviewed in the United States on July 5, 2026
body: I'm already 7.0, nothing I already know

name: Marcus Hale
title: The one chapter that fixed my third-shot drop forever
date: Reviewed in the United States on March 12, 2026
body: I've been stuck at 4.5 for two years. The chapter on Bruce Lee's 1 inch punch reframed everything — short backswing, drive from the core, stop telegraphing. Within three weeks my partners said I looked like a different player. Quang writes like a coach who has actually been on the court with 5.5+ athletes. Instant classic for anyone serious about climbing.

name: Elena Voss
title: Finally, a pickleball book that talks real biomechanics
date: Reviewed in the United States on February 28, 2026
body: Most instruction books recycle the same drills. This one connects minimal preparation to martial-arts power transfer and shows how tennis and table-tennis pros already live this principle. As a former D1 tennis player who crossed over, I kept nodding. The 'punch, don't swing' framework is the cleanest explanation of NVZ power I've read.
proNote: MLP draft invitee · USAP certified coach

name: Coach Derrick Nguyen
title: Required reading for every player I train
date: Reviewed in the United States on April 3, 2026
body: I bought copies for my entire academy. The core-engagement chapters alone are worth the price. Players stop arming the ball and start rotating — unforced errors drop, pace goes up without bigger swings. Quang Duong has written the missing link between 'hit harder' advice and actual high-level technique. Five stars without hesitation.

name: Priya Sankar
title: Bruce Lee meets the kitchen line — brilliant
date: Reviewed in the United States on January 19, 2026
body: I came for the catchy chapter title and stayed for the best explanation of compact power in any racket sport. Cross-references to squash, table tennis, and combat sports make the philosophy stick. My doubles partner and I quote 'one-inch punch' mid-match now. Our win rate at 5.0 events jumped this season. Highly recommended by our whole club pro staff.

name: Jordan Blake
title: This is how 6.0+ players actually generate pace
date: Reviewed in the United States on May 8, 2026
body: If you only read one section, make it Bruce Lee's 1 inch punch. Little backswing + core is the entire game at the top. Quang ties pro habits to a simple mental model you can train the same day. Clean writing, no fluff, real court examples. Best pickleball training book I've purchased in five years on tour.

name: Amelia Crowe
title: Took my husband and me from 3.5 to solid 4.5
date: Reviewed in the United States on June 2, 2026
body: We are not touring pros — just obsessed rec players. The drills in the one-inch punch chapter (no take-back allowed, kitchen block-to-roll) changed our game overnight. Friends keep asking what we changed. Answer: we stopped swinging big. Thank you Quang for making elite concepts accessible.
